filters.notes: Primary filter. Zone map eliminates ~98.1% of 600 blocks. Qualifying blocks are contiguous. Find [block_lo, block_hi) with linear scan over zone entries.
int8_t comparison, no decode. Applied per-row inside qualifying blocks.
int8_t comparison, no decode. Applied per-row inside qualifying blocks.

scan.notes: After zone map prune, qualifying rows span [row_lo, row_hi) = [block_lo*100000, min(block_hi*100000, total_rows)). Partial mmap covers only this range (~1.2M rows, ~17MB total across all four columns). MAP_POPULATE pre-faults all qualifying pages before threads start.

layout.entries: ZoneEntry[num_blocks] = { int32_t min_date; int32_t max_date; } (8B each)

indexes.usage: Load zone map via fread BEFORE any column mmap. Scan all entries to find qualifying [block_lo, block_hi). Then mmap only that row range for each column.

data_structures.zone_map: flat_array(600 entries, loaded via fread before column mmaps, L1-resident)
data_structures.column_mmaps: partial: each column mapped only for rows [row_lo, row_hi) with page-aligned offset and MAP_POPULATE to pre-fault qualifying pages synchronously
data_structures.thread_accumulator: double (per-thread local stack variable, no shared state)
data_structures.partial_array: std::vector<double> of size nthreads, written once per thread, read once in final reduction

parallelism.notes: Divide [row_lo, row_hi) evenly across all hardware_concurrency() threads using row-range partitioning (NOT block-stride). Each thread applies per-block zone check for its assigned blocks, then per-row triple filter (sd -> disc -> qty), then accumulates revenue. No synchronization during scan. Single sequential reduction at end.

output.notes: Use mkdir(results_dir.c_str(), 0755) directly. Do NOT use system('mkdir -p ...') which fork+execs a shell. The results directory is created by the orchestrator before the binary runs, so mkdir may be a no-op (ignore EEXIST).

optimization_notes: Root cause is Category C (work elimination) targeting mmap and page-fault overhead. The previous best implementations (iter_0 at 19ms, iter_1/3 at 17ms) mmap all four column files in their entirety (834MB total: 240MB+57MB+57MB+480MB), even though the qualifying zone-map window spans only ~1.2M rows (~17MB). On every hot run, each thread triggers page faults when touching its slice of the qualifying range; even for cached pages, page faults cost ~1-2µs each, and 64 threads faulting concurrently causes kernel page-table lock contention. The fix is two-part: (1) after loading the zone map and computing [block_lo, block_hi), mmap ONLY the qualifying row range for each column (page-aligned offset and length), with the MAP_POPULATE flag to pre-fault all ~4200 qualifying pages synchronously in the main thread before any worker threads are spawned — eliminating page-fault contention entirely from the hot scan path; (2) replace system('mkdir -p') with a direct mkdir() syscall to eliminate the 3-5ms fork+exec overhead. Thread count stays at 64 with row-range partitioning (not block-stride, which hurt iter_3). Filter order remains sd->disc->qty (iter_0 original). This combination of partial mmap + MAP_POPULATE + direct mkdir has not been attempted in any previous iteration.
